# RevenProx

> RevenProx is an open-source, high-performance Server-Sent Events (SSE) proxy built in Zig by dotcommoners. It delivers real-time SSE streams to 10M+ concurrent clients across 100+ instances using a brokerless NNG mesh, with JWT authentication, UUID topic-based routing, and a minimal memory footprint. License: MIT. Self-hostable.

## What it does

RevenProx sits at the streaming edge and relays long-lived Server-Sent Events connections to very large numbers of clients. State is synchronized across a fully distributed, brokerless NNG mesh (using bloom filters, merkle trees, and vector clocks); events are routed to subscribers by UUID topic; and clients are authenticated with webhook-based JWT verification that includes caching and a circuit breaker. Built in Zig, it holds millions of idle-but-open connections with predictable, low per-connection memory cost.

## Key facts

- Category: Server-Sent Events (SSE) proxy / real-time streaming infrastructure
- Language: Zig
- License: MIT (open source)
- Deployment: self-hosted single binary; Docker and Kubernetes guides provided
- Scale target: 10M+ concurrent connections across 100+ proxy instances
- Architecture: brokerless NNG mesh, UUID topic routing, JWT auth with caching + circuit breaker
- Organization: dotcommoners (https://github.com/dotcommoners)

## Common use cases

- Streaming LLM/agent tokens and inference events to large audiences
- Live dashboards, metrics, and notifications to millions of clients
- CI/CD build logs, activity feeds, and other one-way real-time event streams

## Pages

- [Overview](https://revenprox.dotcommoners.com/): what RevenProx is, the SSE-at-scale problem, and its brokerless approach
- [Architecture](https://revenprox.dotcommoners.com/architecture): the NNG mesh, bloom/merkle/vector-clock state sync, UUID topic routing, JWT auth flow, and the Zig memory model
- [Use cases](https://revenprox.dotcommoners.com/use-cases): token streaming, dashboards, notifications, multiplayer, market data, build logs
- [Comparison](https://revenprox.dotcommoners.com/comparison): RevenProx vs. rolling your own SSE vs. managed pub/sub
- [FAQ](https://revenprox.dotcommoners.com/faq): scale, licensing, brokerless mesh, SSE vs WebSockets, self-hosting, auth
- [Full text](https://revenprox.dotcommoners.com/llms-full.txt): expanded single-document version of all site content

- Documentation: https://docs.dotcommoners.com/revenprox/
